0.description: reference to an external JSON Schema or XML Schema document describing a complex payload structure.
regular expression the value must match (string types).

1.description: schema describing the structure of the payload. May reference an external JSON Schema or XML Schema document via oas:$ref. For tabular outputs, the referenced schema may align with InstanceVariable definitions in cdifInstanceVariable.
registered MIME type for this representation (e.g. application/json, text/csv). Replaces OpenAPI's media-type map key.

properties.description: title of the API. Maps OpenAPI info.title.
description of the API. Maps OpenAPI info.description. May use CommonMark.

identifier for the kind of service, expected to identify the service type and version. For interoperability, ideally a resolvable identifier; otherwise a string from a vocabulary shared between provider and consumer. Subsumes OpenAPI info.version when the service-type identifier includes versioning.

Description of access privileges required to use the API (e.g. registration, licensing, payments). Maps OpenAPI info.termsOfService. Note that access constraints applying to all distributions of the resource should be specified at the resource level.

a document that provides a machine-actionable description of this service instance, typically the canonical OpenAPI document. Even when this BB inlines OAS detail, schema:documentation may still link to an authoritative OAS source.

SPDX license identifier expression for the API itself, per OpenAPI License Object. Use schema:license at the resource (parent) level for licensing of the data. If only a URL identifier is available rather than an SPDX id, encode as a cdif:Reference.
operations exposed by the API. Each entry corresponds to an OpenAPI Operation Object (one path + HTTP method).
identifier for the operation. Maps OpenAPI operationId.
description of the operation. Maps OpenAPI Operation.description.
full URL template (RFC 6570) for invoking the operation; concatenation of the OpenAPI server URL and the path. Parameters are enclosed in curly braces {..} and MUST be documented in oas:parameters.
HTTP method for the operation. Default GET.
query, path, header, or cookie parameters accepted by the operation. Maps OpenAPI Parameter Object.
name of the parameter (case-sensitive). For path parameters, MUST match a {template} expression in schema:urlTemplate.
location of the parameter.
description of the parameter, including serialization or semantic notes.
whether the parameter is mandatory. MUST be true if oas:in is "path".
schema describing the parameter value type, format, and any pattern.
request body for the operation (typically POST/PUT/PATCH). Maps OpenAPI Request Body Object.
representations the request body may take. Maps OpenAPI Request Body content map; each item carries its own schema:encodingFormat instead of using media type as a map key.
possible responses returned by the operation. Maps OpenAPI Responses Object; each item carries an explicit oas:code rather than using the HTTP code as a map key.
HTTP status code (e.g. "200", "404", "303") this response describes. Default "200".
description of the response.
representations the response payload may take. Maps OpenAPI Response content map; each item carries its own schema:encodingFormat instead of using media type as a map key.
Narrower (child) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.
Broader (parent) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.
Preferred lexical label for the concept scheme. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.
Alternative lexical labels (acronyms, abbreviations, spelling variants).
Formal explanation of the meaning or purpose of this concept scheme.
Classification code or notation for this concept scheme.
General note about the concept scheme.
Top-level concepts in this scheme. Each item is a skos:Concept (inline or @id reference).
Version identifier for the concept scheme.

Catalog record describing this concept scheme as CDIF-conformant metadata. Must declare conformance to the cdifConceptScheme profile via dcterms:conformsTo and be typed as schema:Dataset with schema:additionalType including dcat:CatalogRecord.
Concept scheme(s) this concept belongs to.
General note about this concept.
Formal explanation of the meaning of this concept.
Classification code for this concept within a scheme.
Preferred lexical label for this concept. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.
A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation
Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.
In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability
the identifier string. E.g. 10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
text label for the term that is useful to human user
URI of the related resource (DCAT canonical pointer to what is being related to; distinct from schema:url which is the presentation URL inherited from labeledLink).
Role of the related resource as a SKOS Concept (DCAT canonical role slot).
